response:
I am sorry to hear you are experiencing issues with the Zoom connectivity check. To resolve this, please try the following troubleshooting steps:

1. **Check Network Restrictions**: Ensure that your network or firewall is not blocking the following domains:
   - `*.zoom.us`
   - `*.*.zoom.us`
   - `zoom.us`
   If you are on a corporate or university network, you may need to contact your IT administrator to allow these URLs.

2. **Update Your Browser**: This feature is supported on the latest versions of Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, and Mozilla Firefox. Please ensure you are using the most up-to-date version of one of these browsers for the best performance.

3. **Zoom Software**: Please note that no Zoom account or separate software installation is required, as calls connect automatically through the browser when the interview starts.

If the issue persists, you can contact support at support@hackerrank.com for further assistance.
justification: The response is based on the provided documentation regarding Zoom-powered calls in HackerRank Interviews, specifically addressing network prerequisites (safelisting domains) and browser compatibility requirements.

response:

response:
To remove an interviewer from the HackerRank for Work platform, you must remove them from their assigned team. If a user belongs to only one team, removing them from that team will lock the user and prevent access to the platform.

### Steps to Remove a User:
1. Log in to your HackerRank for Work account.
2. Select your profile icon in the upper-right corner.
3. Select Teams Management from the drop-down menu.
4. From here, you can remove the team member.

### Troubleshooting the "Three Dots" Menu:
If you do not see the option to remove the user when selecting the three dots, please check the following:
- Permissions: You must have Company Admin or Team Admin access to manage team members.
- Location: Ensure you are in the Teams Management section. If you are in the Users tab, you may only see options such as "Unlock User" under the Actions menu.
justification: The response provides the correct steps to remove a user by removing them from a team via Teams Management, as specified in the documentation. It also addresses the user's specific issue regarding the missing menu option by explaining the permission requirements (Company Admin/Team Admin) and the difference between the Users tab and Teams Management.

response:
To stop Claude (Anthropic Bots) from crawling your website, you must modify your robots.txt file by adding the following:

User-agent: ClaudeBot
Disallow: /

Please note that blocking IP addresses may not work correctly or persistently guarantee an opt-out, as it prevents the bot from reading your robots.txt file. You can verify if a crawler is from Anthropic by checking the IP list at https://claude.com/crawling/bots.json.

For further questions or to report a malfunctioning bot, please email claudebot@anthropic.com from an email address associated with the domain you are contacting us about.
justification: The response provides the exact robots.txt configuration required to block ClaudeBot as specified in the documentation, warns against IP blocking, provides the IP verification link, and gives the correct contact email for further issues.

response:
Depending on your account and program, the retention period for data used to improve models varies:

- Development Partner Program: Data provided for model training is stored securely for up to two years. If you leave the program, previously provided data will still be stored for up to 2 years.
- General Consumer Data: The default data retention period is 30 days.

To protect your privacy, Claude automatically de-links your data from your user ID (such as your email address) before any review, and access is limited to a small number of personnel involved in model training. You can adjust your privacy settings at any time at https://claude.ai/settings/data-privacy-controls.
justification: The response is based on the provided documentation which specifies a 2-year retention period for the Development Partner Program and a 30-day default retention period for personal data, while also noting the de-linking of user IDs for training data.

To remove an employee from your HackerRank for Work account, please follow these steps:

1. Log in to your HackerRank for Work account.
2. Click on your profile icon in the upper-right corner.
3. Select Teams Management from the drop-down menu.

Prerequisite: You must have Company Admin or Team Admin access to perform these actions.
justification: The response is based on the 'Manage Team Members' and 'Locking user access' documentation, providing the exact steps to access Teams Management and explaining the effect of removing a user from their only team.

response:
To set up the Claude LTI integration for your students, please note that these steps must be performed by Claude for Education administrators and Learning Management Systems (LMS) administrators. If you do not have these permissions, we encourage you to contact your university's administrator(s).

If you have the necessary administrative access, the setup process is as follows:

1. Create the Claude LTI Developer Key in Canvas:
- Sign in as a Canvas administrator and go to Admin -> Developer Keys.
- Click "+ Developer Key" then "+ LTI Key."
- Enter the following details:
  - Key Name: Claude LTI
  - Redirect URIs: https://claude.ai/lti/launch
  - Target Link URI: https://claude.ai/lti/launch
  - OpenID Connect Initiation Url: https://claude.ai/api/lti/login
  - JWK method: https://claude.ai/api/lti/keys
- Set Privacy Level to Public.
- Under Placements, add "Course Navigation" and "Assignment Edit."
- Save and toggle the state to On.

2. Enable the integration in Claude for Education:
- Sign in as an administrator and navigate to Organization settings > Connectors.
- Find Canvas and click "Enable."
- Input the Canvas Domain, Client ID (from Canvas Admin -> Developer Keys), and Deployment ID (from Canvas Admin -> Settings -> Apps -> View App Configurations -> Claude LTI Settings Button -> Deployment ID).
- Click "Save Changes."
justification: The response provides the exact steps from the documentation for setting up the Claude LTI in Canvas, while correctly noting that these actions require administrator privileges and advising the professor to contact their university administrator if they lack those permissions.

response: In the US Virgin Islands, merchants are permitted to require a minimum transaction amount of US$10, but this exception applies only to credit cards.
